title: Dal Makhani
description: Slow-cooked black lentils and kidney beans enriched with butter, cream, tomato, and gentle spices.

photos.credit: From the original Singh's Connoisseur Corner website

steps: Soak the lentils and kidney beans overnight, then drain.
Pressure-cook with salt and water until very soft; let the pressure release naturally.
Simmer the cooked lentils on low heat while preparing the masala.
Heat butter and ghee, sauté grated onion until golden, then add ginger-garlic paste.
Add tomato purée and cook until the fat separates.
Stir in the lentils, spices, salt, and water. Simmer uncovered for about 45 minutes, stirring often.
Add sugar, remaining butter, and cream. Simmer 10 minutes more and serve hot.
notes: For smokiness, use the optional dhungar charcoal method.
Imported from the original Singh's Connoisseur Corner recipe collection.
